posts.name: Pirom P.
posts.cooked:
Hi does anyone know if Roulot’s Marc and FIne de Bourgogne are worth buying?

I’m quite intrigued on this and but tasting notes and information on how its made out there is scarce.

Also anyone know the difference between his Marc de Bourgogne des Annees 50 and the regular Marc is?

posts.name: A. So
posts.cooked:
If I remember correctly, marc is distilled from the pomace, fine from the fine lees. I’ve had various versions of these distillates (including DRC’s) and they’re never worth the money to me (get a cognac, armagnac, calvados, etc. instead).

The Roulot apricot liqueur on the other hand…

posts.name: Barry L i p t o n
posts.cooked: Any more on the Roulot apricot liquer? Worth the tariff?
posts.display_username: Barry L i p t o n

posts.name: Ilkka |_ |_| mme
posts.cooked:
Any more on the Roulot apricot liquer? Worth the tariff?

I have no idea what the tariff might at retail but I’ve had it in a restaurant for something like 6€ a serving and it is extremely delicious.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked:
Hi does anyone know if Roulot’s Marc and FIne de Bourgogne are worth buying?

I’m quite intrigued on this and but tasting notes and information on how its made out there is scarce.

Also anyone know the difference between his Marc de Bourgogne des Annees 50 and the regular Marc is?

If you like marc and fine they are! The marc “des Années 50” comes from the 1950s, the regular marc isn’t as old.

Probably the best marc I ever tasted is Leroy’s…

The best QPR that I know of and a very good example is from Domaine de la Folie in Rully.

posts.name: Barry L i p t o n
posts.cooked: The Roulot apricot liquer seems to be $110-$120 for the most part if not on sale.
posts.display_username: Barry L i p t o n

posts.name: Marshall Manning
posts.cooked: Chevillon used to make a marc and nice fine…the marc was pretty rustic, and would put hair on your chest.

posts.name: Asger L a r s e n
posts.cooked:
no experience tasting this, only the DRC that I do like, if today I find too expensive

Remember a tasting at Roulot domaine 10 years ago. Fantastic range of Meursault wines, walking in the cellars I noticed a good amount of Marc in bottles.

your post do bring back happy memories
posts.display_username: Asger L a r s e n

posts.name: Andrew K0t0wski
posts.cooked:
The Roulot apricot liquer seems to be $110-$120 for the most part if not on sale.

That’s the price point I’ve seen it at. If you are open to apricot liqueur, it’s spectacular.

posts.name: Barry L i p t o n
posts.cooked: Morrell has the Abricot for $84 but doesn’t ship to California
posts.display_username: Barry L i p t o n

posts.cooked:
What’s the going rate in the US for these?

I recently picked up some of the newer Roulot (black labels) Marc and Fine at auction in Europe for about £35. Still sitting in the UK but I think shipping to US is ~$20 USD.

Seems like the Marc goes for a (very) small premium over the Fine but the Fine has better reviews.

posts.name: D Borczon
posts.cooked:
The Roulot apricot liquer seems to be $110-$120 for the most part if not on sale.

Unfortunate as I bought a bottle last year in Beaune and it was $60.00. Best bargain in Mon Millesime. Yes it is delicious.

posts.name: Barry L i p t o n
posts.cooked:
Yes it is delicious but I find a tiny bit goes a long way. A liter will last 20 years at this rate…

Any cocktail recipe’s using the apricot liquer?
posts.display_username: Barry L i p t o n
